title: Five Deep Breaths
synopsis: When a small forest sprite finds himself trapped inside a grandmother's cozy house, he must learn to breathe through his fear and discover that the wrinkled human isn't so scary after all.

pages.text:
THUMP goes the door behind the little sprite!

'Oh my stars!' says Grandmother, flour puffing from her hands like tiny clouds. 'What do we have here?'

Sprout's glowing yellow eyes grow wide, wide, WIDE! His twig arms shake like leaves in a storm. The house smells like cinnamon swirls and dusty book pages, but all he wants is OUT, OUT, OUT!

'I need to go!' squeaks Sprout, his leafy green skin trembling. 'I don't belong here! I don't belong here!'

Grandmother stops her kneading. PLOP goes the dough. 'Well now,' she says softly, 'let's count together. Can you take five deep breaths with me?'

'Five!' gasps Sprout, his acorn-cap hat wobbling. 'Five breaths before I can go?'

The ivy around his shoulders rustles nervously. Outside the window, his forest home seems so far away.

'Five deep breaths,' Grandmother nods, 'and we'll see what happens next.'

Breathe in... breathe out... one breath down, four to go!

illustration.description: A small mossy forest sprite with leafy green skin, acorn-cap hat, twig arms, glowing yellow eyes, and ivy wrapped around his shoulders stands pressed against a closed wooden door, looking panicked, while a gentle grandmother with flour-dusted hands and apron smiles kindly at him in a warm kitchen filled with golden light, cinnamon steam, and shelves of old books
illustration.alt: Sprout trapped inside grandmother's kitchen, looking scared

pages.text:
SNIFF, SNIFF goes Sprout's tiny nose.

'What's that smell?' he whispers, his twig arms still shaking but not quite so much. 'It's like... like golden sunshine in the air!'

Grandmother chuckles, a sound like wind chimes dancing. 'That's my cinnamon rolls, little forest friend. Been baking since sunrise, I have.'

Sprout takes another breath - deeper this time. His leafy green skin starts to feel less prickly. The kitchen is warm, so warm, like being wrapped in a fuzzy blanket.

'I still want to go home,' Sprout says, but his voice is softer now. 'My mushroom house misses me. My tree friends are wondering where I am!'

'Four more breaths,' Grandmother says, rolling her dough with gentle THUMP-THUMP sounds. 'Tell me about your tree friends while we breathe.'

Sprout's glowing eyes blink once, twice. 'They whisper secrets when the wind blows,' he says quietly.

Breathe in the cinnamon... breathe out the worry... two breaths down, three to go!

illustration.description: A small mossy forest sprite with leafy green skin, acorn-cap hat, twig arms, glowing yellow eyes, and ivy wrapped around his shoulders stands closer to the kitchen counter, sniffing the air with curiosity while grandmother rolls out cinnamon roll dough, golden steam swirling between them in the cozy kitchen
illustration.alt: Sprout beginning to relax, smelling cinnamon rolls baking

pages.text:
TICK-TOCK, TICK-TOCK goes the old wooden clock.

Sprout finds himself stepping closer to Grandmother's floury workspace. His acorn-cap hat tilts with curiosity. 'Your hands look like tree bark,' he whispers, 'all wrinkled and wise.'

Grandmother laughs her wind-chime laugh. 'And your skin looks like the moss on my favorite reading rock! See? We're not so different, you and I.'

Sprout touches the ivy around his shoulders. It feels less tight now, more like a friendly hug than a worried squeeze. 'But you're BIG and I'm small. You're old and I'm... well, I'm old too, but forest-old. Different-old!'

'Different can be delightful,' Grandmother says, sprinkling sugar that sparkles like fairy dust. SPRINKLE, SPARKLE, SHINE!

Sprout takes his third deep breath. The kitchen doesn't feel like a trap anymore. It feels like... like a different kind of cozy.

'Three breaths down,' he counts on his twig fingers. 'Two to go, then I can choose what happens next.'

Breathe in the kindness... breathe out the fear... getting calmer, getting braver!

illustration.description: A small mossy forest sprite with leafy green skin, acorn-cap hat, twig arms, glowing yellow eyes, and ivy wrapped around his shoulders stands right beside the kitchen counter, comparing his small twig hand to grandmother's gentle, wrinkled hand dusted with flour and sugar sparkles
illustration.alt: Sprout and grandmother comparing their different but similar hands

pages.text:
DING! goes the oven timer, bright and cheerful.

'Perfect timing!' Grandmother claps her floury hands together. CLAP-PUFF-CLAP! 'The first batch is ready!'

Sprout's glowing yellow eyes watch as she opens the oven door. WHOOSH! Out comes the most amazing smell - like hugs and happiness all rolled into golden spirals.

'They look like tiny suns!' Sprout gasps, his fear melting away like butter on warm bread. 'Can I... can I help with the next ones?'

Grandmother's smile grows bigger than a harvest moon. 'I was hoping you'd ask! These old hands could use a helper.'

Sprout climbs onto a kitchen stool - CLATTER-SCRAMBLE-UP! His twig arms reach for the bowl of cinnamon. The ivy around his shoulders sways happily.

'I've never baked with a human before,' Sprout admits, stirring with careful little circles. 'In the forest, we just eat berries and morning dew.'

'And I've never baked with a forest sprite before!' Grandmother says. 'What a wonderful first for both of us!'

Two more breaths... almost there... almost ready to decide!

illustration.description: A small mossy forest sprite with leafy green skin, acorn-cap hat, twig arms, glowing yellow eyes, and ivy wrapped around his shoulders stands on a wooden kitchen stool helping grandmother stir cinnamon filling, both of them smiling as golden cinnamon rolls cool on the counter nearby
illustration.alt: Sprout helping grandmother bake, both working together happily

pages.text:
BUBBLE-POP goes the tea kettle, singing its steamy song.

'One last breath,' Grandmother says gently, 'and then you can choose. Stay for tea and cinnamon rolls, or I'll open that door and you can scamper back to your forest home.'

Sprout sits cross-legged on the kitchen table, his leafy green skin warm in the golden light. His acorn-cap hat sits perfectly straight now, no more wobbling with worry.

He takes the deepest breath of all - IN with the cinnamon and kindness, OUT with the last little bit of scared.

'You know what?' Sprout says, his glowing eyes twinkling like fireflies. 'I think... I think I'd like to stay for tea. Just for a little while.'

Grandmother pours two cups - one big, one sprite-sized. GLUG-GLUG-PERFECT!

'But after tea,' Sprout adds, 'could I maybe bring my tree friends to meet you? I think they'd like your cinnamon magic too.'

'I would love that more than anything,' Grandmother says, settling into her chair with a happy sigh.

One breath left... but now it's not about leaving. Now it's about beginning!

illustration.description: A small mossy forest sprite with leafy green skin, acorn-cap hat, twig arms, glowing yellow eyes, and ivy wrapped around his shoulders sits contentedly at a cozy kitchen table across from grandmother, both holding steaming teacups with fresh cinnamon rolls between them, warm afternoon light streaming through lace curtains
illustration.alt: Sprout and grandmother sharing tea and cinnamon rolls together

morals: Taking time to breathe helps with fear

coverPrompt.description: A small mossy forest sprite with leafy green skin, acorn-cap hat, twig arms, glowing yellow eyes, and ivy wrapped around his shoulders stands nervously by a window inside a warm kitchen filled with cinnamon scents and golden light, while a gentle grandmother's hands can be seen kneading dough in the background
coverPrompt.alt: Forest sprite Sprout looking anxious inside grandmother's kitchen

questions.question: What does Sprout wear on his head?
questions.explanation: Sprout wears an acorn-cap hat that wobbles when he's nervous!

questions.question: What is Grandmother making in her kitchen?
questions.explanation: Grandmother is making cinnamon rolls that smell like golden sunshine!

questions.question: How many deep breaths does Grandmother ask Sprout to take?
questions.explanation: Grandmother asks Sprout to take five deep breaths to help him feel calmer.

questions.question: What does Sprout decide to do at the end?
questions.explanation: After taking five deep breaths, Sprout decides to stay for tea and cinnamon rolls!

questions.question: What does Sprout want to bring to meet Grandmother?
questions.explanation: Sprout wants to bring his tree friends to meet Grandmother and try her cinnamon magic!
